mnemonics: NAIVE BAYES IS FAST: Not Always Ideal, Very Efficient & Basic Assumptions Inherently Simple
SPAM HAM SENTIMENT: Simple Probabilistic Algorithm Masters Handling All Messages, Sentiment Especially, Not Too complex
keyPoints: Naive Bayes can be adapted for tasks beyond spam filtering, such as sentiment analysis, topic categorization, and author identification.
The 'naive' assumption of feature independence simplifies calculations and often works well for text despite the inherent dependence between words.
It serves as a strong, computationally efficient baseline model against which more complex algorithms (like SVMs or Neural Networks) are compared.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the data preprocessing step (like stop word removal or stemming), which is crucial for good performance in text classification.
Forgetting to apply Laplace (add-one) smoothing, leading to zero probabilities for unseen words in the test data and breaking the model.
Misinterpreting the model's probability outputs as well-calibrated, confident probabilities rather than just scores for classification.
